blocks.body: Some / Any helps you use words for indefinite place: somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere.

blocks.heading: Core idea
blocks.body:
• This section centres on the words somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere.
  • They help you talk about an indefinite place, no place, any place or every place.

blocks.heading: Using somewhere
blocks.body:
• Somewhere is common in affirmative sentences: I left my bag somewhere upstairs.
  • In a question, it can sound more expected or more specific: Did you find somewhere to sit?

blocks.heading: Using anywhere and nowhere
blocks.body:
• Anywhere is typical in questions and negatives: Do you want to go anywhere? / I can't go anywhere.
  • Nowhere gives the negative meaning in one word, without extra not.

blocks.heading: Place words
blocks.body:
TopicLevelDescription
Some / Any — PlacesA2somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere in statements, questions and negatives

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with somewhere for affirmative sentences and anywhere for questions and negatives.

Nowhere already carries the negative meaning, while everywhere means “in every place”.
